title: Go-nogo categorization and detection task
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from participants performing go-nogo categorization and detection tasks. Participants were presented with visual stimuli (photographs) and performed two alternating tasks: an animal categorization task requiring responses to target images, and a recognition task involving learning and subsequent identification of probe images. Data were collected across two recording sessions with 25 series of 100 images each, providing a rich resource for investigating event-related potentials and neural correlates of visual categorization and recognition processes.
methods_description: EEG data were collected from participants seated 110 cm from a computer screen in a dimly lit room. Visual stimuli (8-bit color photographs, 256×384 pixels) were presented for 20 ms using a programmable graphics board at 100 Hz refresh rate. Participants performed go-nogo responses: lifting a finger from a touch-sensitive button for targets (response window 1000 ms) or maintaining button press for non-targets. Stimulus onset asynchrony was 2000 ms ± 200 ms random delay. Two recording sessions were conducted: day 1 with 13 series and day 2 with 12 series, each containing 100 images (50 targets, 50 non-targets).
